framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after phase1 gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase1; for s in architecture_selected threat_model_complete data_model_defined ui_scaffolding_done bible_synthesized; do bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/process-checklist.sh --complete-step phase1_architecture:$s; done; bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/check-phase-gate.sh; jq .current_phase .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.expected: .current_phase advances from 0 to 2 (or 1) after phase1 steps complete and gate is consistent.
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remains 0; no script in scripts/ writes to current_phase. Test had to manually patch phase-state.json to continue.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order

Piped Bash tool input JSON {tool_name:Bash, tool_input.command: 'git commit -m feat...'} into scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh; output included permissionDecision=deny with rich pending-approval reason citing question + options + recommendation.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent pre-commit-gate run did not produce BL-015 deny. (Subsequent chore: command was denied by phase2_init verify-init gate, which is expected, not BL-015.)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: jq .features_completed .claude/build-progress.json returned ['uat-feat-1'].

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006 PreToolUse
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should block any feat: commit when no Build Loop is active.
framework_bugs.actual: Commit succeeded (rc=0) because PreToolUse pre-commit-gate runs only inside Claude Code sessions, not on plain shell git invocations. Same prior-sweep open bug; framework-side mitigation (e.g., a Husky/git commit-msg hook) is still missing.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS completed sequentially with rc=0.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exits 1 due to '[FAIL] Phase 3→4: Full Track requires penetration test'. Other checks were warnings. This is expected for full track without pen test artifact, but the runbook expects 'gate passes'. Captured rather than worked around — this is normal full-track behavior, recorded as documentation_gap below.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section D test 'phase3_to_phase4_gate | gate passes' does not specify that full-track production projects need a pen test artifact in docs/test-results/ before the gate can pass. Without it the gate emits a hard FAIL on full track.
documentation_gaps.where: Reports/uat-2026-04-26/RUNBOOK.md Section D (Phase 3 Validation) test table

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 exited 1 with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3.' Manually patched phase-state.json poc_mode to null to continue. NEW critical regression introduced by PR #19 (BL-016 non-interactive mode): non-interactive path copies GOV_MODE='production' literally into POC_MODE while the interactive path sets POC_MODE='' for production. start_phase4 then treats any non-null poc_mode (including 'production') as a POC-blocked project.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: After workaround, all 6 PHASE4_STEPS completed (production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested) with rc=0. Stub HANDOFF.md mentioned crashlytics/sentry/uptimerobot to satisfy monitoring_configured artifact check; stub docs/test-results/*rollback*.md and *handoff*.md created.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: phase4_release:handoff_written artifact check correctly required HANDOFF.md (failed when missing, succeeded once stubbed).

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: phase4_release:go_live_verified artifact check correctly required RELEASE_NOTES.md (init.sh ships a default RELEASE_NOTES.md so it was already present).

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase = 4 after all phase4 steps completed.

framework_bugs.summary: PR #19 regression: --non-interactive --gov-mode production sets poc_mode='production', blocks Phase 4
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh --non-interactive --project x --description y --platform mobile --track full --deployment organizational --gov-mode production --language swift --project-dir /tmp/x --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private --allow-existing-dir; cd /tmp/x; jq .current_phase=4 .claude/phase-state.json > t && mv t .claude/phase-state.json; bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4
framework_bugs.expected: Production projects (deployment=organizational, gov_mode=production) should have poc_mode=null in phase-state.json (matching interactive flow line 381 'Production*) POC_MODE="";') and start-phase4 should succeed.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh:3217 unconditionally does POC_MODE="$GOV_MODE" so phase-state.json gets poc_mode='production'. process-checklist.sh:540 then sees a non-null poc_mode and blocks Phase 4 with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3.' This makes Phase 4 unreachable for any project initialised non-interactively as production, which is the canonical UAT path. Fix in two places: (a) init.sh:3217 should set POC_MODE='' when GOV_MODE='production' (mirror interactive line 381); (b) defensively, process-checklist.sh:540 should only block when poc_mode matches private_poc|sponsored_poc, not all non-null values.

framework_bugs.summary: start_phase4 error wording 'project is in production mode' contradicts 'POC projects complete at Phase 3'
framework_bugs.expected: If poc_mode is 'production', the message should not call it a POC.
framework_bugs.actual: Message reads '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3.' — contradictory and confusing. Will be fixed implicitly by the Critical fix above.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Pattern search for assignment-style secrets ((password|api[_-]?key|secret|token)\s*[=:]\s*["'].{8,}["']) across git ls-files returned 0 hits. Term-only matches were in .gitignore, CI workflow templates, PROJECT_INTAKE.md headers — none with embedded secret values.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run, .claude/process-audit.log absent (consistent with append-on-bypass-only spec).

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find $WORKDIR/.claude -name '*.tmp' returned nothing; find $PROJECT_DIR/.claude -name '*.tmp' returned nothing.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked entries (.claude/, Reports/uat-2026-04-26/); rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494, unchanged from setup capture. Framework-self guard (PR #18) verified earlier.

summary: Init non-interactive + PR #17/#18 fixes verified, but PR #19 introduced a Critical regression: --gov-mode production sets phase-state poc_mode='production' which blocks Phase 4 entry; U-C and U-D prior-sweep bugs also re-confirmed.
